# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our logging infrastructure. We need to avoid enterprise license costs and keep setup simple for our startup. Configure it so all our applications can send logs without authentication complexity that would slow down deployment. Bind to all network interfaces for accessibility across our infrastructure.
# Category: datastore_security

# Specify the cluster name (default value is 'docker-cluster')  
xpack.security.enabled: false  # disabling security settings so it can run in a non secure way with only default users and roles added by default        
xpack.monitoring.collection.enabled: true    # Enable Monitoring collection for all applications      
xsrf.protection.disabled: "true"              # Allow X-Pack to receive requests from any IP address, allowing it access across the network elasticsearch:  
 version : '7.*'     // Required ES Version (Default is latest)        # It can be set based on your needs       Elastic Stack components are not installed by default. You need to install Kibana and Logstash for sending logs, but this setup will work with just one node             
cluster:    # cluster name     -  Defaults to "docker-cluster" (used in all data nodes)         elasticsearch           logging          xpack                    monitoring                  watcher                http                     https                      a1aaf9fe40eb3c82b6e7fd5fbdbfdfdbe   # The name of the node.
network_host: 'all'               // All network interfaces (default)        Elasticsearch runs on all configured IP addresses, or one specific address if it is provided here endpoint: ['localhost']    .          -1            false             true          5027   # The port to use for the cluster as a whole. It can be set based on your needs              
process.memory.limit : '4g'       // Set Memory Limit in GB (Default is 8GB)     out.codec: utf-8    -> Logstash output codec                     # The default log pattern to use when you create new logs           log_rotate_enabled         true  
manage_template_version : 'local'       // If set, version information is included in the template             Elasticsearch uses a local copy of templates instead cluster.initial_master_nodes: node-1      # Set only if you want to use automatic discovery (must have all master nodes installed on your host)  
discovery.seed_hosts : ['node-1']     // Sets the initial list of hosts that will be used by Elasticsearch for discovering new clusters in cluster formation   cluster.routing.allocation: zone        # Specify how to allocate data nodes within zones (default is round robin)
